hero.eyebrow: Mainland Tanzania · the heart of what we do
hero.lead: From the Serengeti's rolling plains to the Ngorongoro Crater's lost world, we run small-group safaris with the rangers, pilots, and lodge owners we've known for years. No bus tours. No half-truths. Just the bush, well done.

intro.title: Tanzania-bred guides who know the bush — and the islands.
intro.lead: We run the northern circuit — Serengeti, Ngorongoro, Tarangire — with bush flights so you spend more time on game drives and less in a Land Cruiser. Pair with a few days on Zanzibar's coast, or stay pure bush. Every camp is hand-picked. Every guide is Silver-level certified or higher.

bullets.vehicles_text: Max 4 guests per Land Cruiser, every seat a window seat. No mini-buses.
bullets.flights_text: Skip the 8-hour drive. Cessnas connect parks in under an hour.
bullets.fees_text: Park fees, ranger fees, community levies — never tacked on later.

types.title: Different safari styles for different travellers.
types.lead: From classic game drives and fly-in circuits to walking, boat, family, and bush-beach combinations — we tailor each route to your travel style.

modes.dedicated_text: Safari-only booking: arrive in Arusha and we'll pick you up for the northern or southern circuit.
modes.last_minute_text: Already on Zanzibar? We can run 1 day, 1 night, 2 nights, or longer safari options — fast.
modes.package_text: Combined with hotels and excursions in one seamless itinerary.

classic-game-drive.title: Classic game-drive safaris
classic-game-drive.desc: Private or small-group Land Cruiser drives across Serengeti, Ngorongoro, Tarangire, and Lake Manyara with expert local guides.

fly-in.title: Fly-in safaris
fly-in.desc: Skip long road transfers and connect key parks by bush flight from Zanzibar, Arusha, or Dar es Salaam.

walking.title: Walking safaris
walking.desc: Guided bush walks with armed rangers in wilderness areas like Nyerere and Ruaha for a close-to-nature experience.

boat.title: Boat safaris
boat.desc: River and lake safaris in Nyerere (Selous) and western circuits where wildlife is viewed from the water.

family.title: Family safaris
family.desc: Family-friendly itineraries with shorter drives, flexible pacing, and lodges suited for children and multi-gen travel.

luxury-lodge-tented.title: Luxury lodge & tented safaris
luxury-lodge-tented.desc: High-comfort stays, premium camps, and curated service while staying close to migration routes and big-cat territory.

budget-camping.title: Budget camping safaris
budget-camping.desc: Value-focused routes using well-run camps and essential comforts without compromising guiding quality.

bush-beach.title: Bush + beach combinations
bush-beach.desc: Seamless Tanzania mainland safari paired with Zanzibar beach stays, with all flights and transfers coordinated.

parks.title: Five parks. Each its own world.

serengeti.name: Serengeti National Park
serengeti.blurb: The endless plains. Home to the great migration — 1.5 million wildebeest and 250,000 zebra moving in lockstep with the rains.

ngorongoro.name: Ngorongoro Crater
ngorongoro.blurb: A collapsed volcano, now a self-contained ecosystem with the densest concentration of predators in Africa. Black rhino still roam.

tarangire.name: Tarangire National Park
tarangire.blurb: Elephant capital. Ancient baobabs and a year-round river that draws giant herds in the dry season. Wildly underrated.

nyerere.name: Nyerere (Selous)
nyerere.blurb: Africa's largest game reserve. Boat safaris on the Rufiji, walking with armed rangers, fly-camping under impossible stars.

manyara.name: Lake Manyara
manyara.blurb: Tree-climbing lions, alkaline flats stained pink with flamingos, and one of East Africa's great birding spots — 400+ species.

seasons.title: There's no bad time. Just different ones.

peak.title: Dry & classic
peak.blurb: Animals cluster at waterholes. The Mara River crossings happen July–September. Cool, golden grass, picture-postcard. Books out 6+ months ahead.

good.title: Calving season
good.blurb: Wildebeest drop half a million calves on the southern Serengeti plains in February. Predator action peaks. Hot, but lush. Quieter parks.

shoulder.title: Green & cheap
shoulder.blurb: Short rains. Skies are dramatic, parks empty, prices drop 20–30%. Birding peaks in November. Some bush roads can muddy.

avoid.title: Long rains
avoid.blurb: Many camps close. Roads flood. Bargain-hunters only — and even then, we usually steer guests elsewhere this season.

wildlife.title: What you might see.
wildlife.lead: Real photos from real game drives, taken by our guides this past season — Ngorongoro and Serengeti, mostly. No stock library.

rhino-on-plains.webp.cap: Black rhino · <30 left in the crater

compare.title: Choose by what you want most.
compare.lead: Most guests do not know the park names yet. Start with the outcome, then open the route that fits.

tarangire-day-trip.label: Best quick taste
tarangire-day-trip.note: Lowest commitment, real mainland wildlife in one day.

ngorongoro-overnight.label: Best short safari
ngorongoro-overnight.note: One night, high wildlife density, crater at first light.

ngorongoro-tarangire.label: Best first-timer route
ngorongoro-tarangire.note: Classic Big Five scenery plus elephants and baobabs.

serengeti-migration.label: Best migration focus
serengeti-migration.note: For river-crossing season and big open-plains drama.

nyerere-selous.label: Best remote wilderness
nyerere-selous.note: Fewer vehicles, wilder camps, stronger adventure feel.

mahale-chimp.label: Best premium add-on
mahale-chimp.note: Rare chimp trekking with a lakefront finish.

steps.title: Three steps from idea to confirmed safari.

01.title: Choose a route
01.text: Start with a card, or send your dates and we'll recommend the best safari for your budget.

02.title: We price the real trip
02.text: We check flights, camp availability, park fees, and seasonality before sending the final quote.

03.title: Deposit confirms it
03.text: Your deposit locks the flights, guide, camps, and transfers. The rest is paid before travel.

included.title: One price. Nothing surprise-charged.
included.lead: We learnt a long time ago that "from $X" with twelve add-ons makes guests miserable. Our quotes include everything below.

cta.ai_planner: Or chat with our AI planner

days.q: How many days do I need on safari?
days.a: Three nights is the absolute minimum and only worth it if you fly between parks. Five nights is the sweet spot. Anything over seven and you'll start to crave the beach — which is why most guests pair safari with Zanzibar.

kids.q: Is it safe to bring kids?
kids.a: Yes — most camps welcome children 7+. We pick "family" lodges with pools and flexible game drives. For kids under 7, we suggest Tarangire (shorter drives, big elephant sightings) over Serengeti.

malaria.a: Tanzania is a malaria area. Speak to your travel doctor about prophylaxis. Camps provide repellent, mosquito nets, and most have screened tents.

big_five.a: In the Ngorongoro Crater, you'll usually tick four of five in a single day. Leopard is the wildcard — sometimes the same morning, sometimes never. We don't promise sightings; the bush isn't a zoo. We do promise we'll work hard to find them.

camera.q: What kind of camera should I bring?
camera.a: A 200mm zoom minimum if you want frame-fillers. Most of our guests now shoot phones with a small clip-on telephoto and they do great. Bring a dust-blower — every camp's nightmare is sand on the sensor.
